ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'croissant' class (FOODON_03302114); no specific class exists for torched-cheese-topped butter croissant. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers croissant as a pastry category; no sub-code exists for cheese-topped or torched variant. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for torched cheese butter croissant; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard torched cheese butter croissant weighing approximately 90 g, as typically served at Singaporean bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Torched Cheese Butter Croissant — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Torched Cheese Butter Croissant (cr_id G00011006) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central butter croissant profile (USDA SR Legacy #18023) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Croissant (GI 67)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for torched cheese butter croissant. GI 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain croissant (Atkinson FS et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2008; Foster-Powell et al. 2002), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 67 × available carbohydrate per serving (45.8 g × 0.9 g serving fraction for 90 g portion = 41.2 g available carb) / 100 = 27.6 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g combined with high saturated fat and low potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this item; sodium at 300 mg/100 g from butter, cheese sauce, and salt, combined with minimal potassium (118 mg/100 g) and low dietary fibre offset, creates an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio that elevates blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (45.8 g/100 g), elevated sugar (11.26 g/100 g), and high saturated fat impairing insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this item or consume only very small portions infrequently; the high glycaemic load (GL ~28 per serving), refined flour base, 11.26 g sugar per 100 g, and approximately 12 g saturated fat per 100 g from butter and cheese collectively worsen postprandial glucose excursions and insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12 g/100 g), cholesterol (67 mg/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and sodium (300 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item; the combination of approximately 12 g saturated fat, 67 mg cholesterol, 0.1 g trans fat, and 300 mg sodium per 100 g significantly raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk, both established drivers of adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12 g/100 g) and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) from torching
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item; the high saturated fat from butter and processed cheese exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold, and the torching process generates AGEs on the cheese surface that further promote joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Torched Cheese Butter Croissant is a Singaporean bakery item that combines a classic French-style laminated butter croissant with a rich cheese sauce made from cream cheese, cheddar cheese slices, and whipping cream, finished by torching the cheese topping to create a caramelised, slightly smoky crust. The croissant base is made from wheat flour (T45 or similar), fine sugar, salt, and generous quantities of butter, producing the characteristic crispy, flaky exterior and soft, airy interior of a well-laminated pastry. The torched cheese topping adds a savoury, umami-rich contrast to the buttery sweetness of the croissant, with a slightly charred, molten texture on the surface. Nutritionally, this item is energy-dense at 406 kcal per 100 g, with 21 g total fat (approximately 12 g saturated), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 8.2 g protein, and 300 mg sodium per 100 g. The glycaemic index is estimated at 67 (medium) based on the analogous plain croissant from the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 28 per standard 90 g serving. Clinically, this item is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high saturated fat, sodium, refined carbohydrate, and AGE-generating preparation method. Best consumed as an occasional treat by healthy adults.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a cheese croissant?
Is butter croissant high in sodium?
What is the GI of a croissant?
Is cheese croissant suitable for hypertension?
Torched cheese croissant nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for this exact product (cr_id G00011006) with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy butter croissant profile as HPB does not separately report saturated fat; this introduces minor uncertainty but does not affect the high confidence classification for the primary macros.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific torched cheese butter croissant is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel not reported in HPB Foodo for this item and no sufficiently close approved-source equivalent was available to estimate these values with confidence. trans_fat_g reported as 0.1 g/100 g based on typical butter-based laminated pastry profile from USDA SR Legacy #18023 (croissant, butter); this is an estimate as HPB does not separately report trans fat. saturated_fat_g of 12.0 g/100 g estimated from USDA SR Legacy butter croissant profile (#18023) adjusted for the additional cheese sauce component. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as GI 67 × 41.2 g available carbohydrate (90 g serving × 45.8 g carb/100 g) / 100 = 27.6. Cultural classification as Singaporean reflects the HPB provenance and Singapore-market origin of this product; the croissant form itself is of French origin.
